svg.title: cloud-ai-security-skills — detection and posture coverage matrix
svg.desc: Coverage matrix visualizing the gap between shipped detection and posture skills and their paired remediation, with ATT&CK or ATLAS technique IDs where the detector emits them and framework notes where it does not. Rows list every shipped detection (35) plus every CSPM benchmark family (4 native CIS scopes). Columns are: skill name, mapping or note, detector or check shipped status, paired remediation status, and the tracking issue or notes. Green cells mean a closed loop is shipped; amber cells mean a remediation skill is on the roadmap; red cells mean no remediation exists or is planned. Thirteen of twenty-nine detection rows are closed loops today; lateral-movement is intentionally detection-only because the response is per-arm fan-out via existing remediation skills, and the access-key, login-profile, GCP service-account-key, GCP service-account-token-minting, discovery-burst, cross-account-copy, logging-impairment, AWS and GCP model-artifact-download, MCP credential-leak, system-prompt-extraction, tool-output-policy-bypass, and tool-output-exfiltration-instructions slices are still detection-first. The matrix is the single source of truth for closed-loop coverage and updates whenever a remediation skill ships.
svg.text: Every shipped detection and posture check, mapped to whether a paired remediation exists, is planned, or is missing.
Green rows are closed loops. MITRE IDs are shown where emitted; OWASP/MCP notes appear where the detector is intentionally non-MITRE.

g.text: detection-first AWS IAM-user access-key persistence slice
detection-first AWS IAM-user console persistence slice
detection-first GCP service-account key persistence slice
detection-first GCP service-account token persistence slice
detection-first AWS cloud discovery burst slice
detection-first AWS exfiltration to cloud account slice
detection-first AWS AI model-artifact collection slice
detection-first GCP AI model-artifact collection slice
detection-only — ClickHouse bulk row export slice (#436)
CIS K8s · NIST SSDF · OWASP LLM

Closed-loop ratio: 13 / 35 · lateral-movement stays detection-only by design, and the AWS access-key, login-profile, GCP service-account-key/token-minting, discovery, exfiltration, logging, AWS and GCP model-artifact collection, MCP leakage, system-prompt, policy-bypass, response-exfiltration, Snowflake bulk-egress, ClickHouse bulk-export, and Databricks token-creation slices are detection-first today
All writes HITL-gated, dry-run-default, dual-audited (DynamoDB + KMS S3, before + after) · contract enforced by scripts/validate_safe_skill_bar.py · CSPM auto-remediation tracked at #242 #254
